title: Development Process — how projects move from idea to built, reviewed work
public_claim: A public explanation of how projects are organized and developed across the portfolio.
distinctive_mechanism: It maps the route from idea generation to source gathering, project room creation, instrument selection, review, release, and status tracking.
use_value: It helps a first-time reader understand that the site is not a pile of pages but a project-development system with repeatable stages.

minimum_public_demo: Test one representative case; confirm input (policy problem, governance failure, oversight gap...) produces output (specific problem the architecture is meant to govern) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.
claim_ceiling: The page may claim proof-structured components and visible verification targets. It may not claim external validation, production deployment, or completed empirical findings unless the project-specific record states those facts.
